modules.name: Memorial General Hospital
modules.description: A simulated hospital network with a staff portal, patient database, and internal workstation.

containers.name: Web / App Server
containers.description: The hospital staff portal. This is the attacker's entry point.

vulnerabilities.name: SQL Injection
vulnerabilities.description: Login form is vulnerable to SQL injection, allowing authentication bypass.

vulnerabilities.name: Directory Traversal
vulnerabilities.description: File endpoint allows traversal outside the web root to read arbitrary files.

vulnerabilities.name: Weak OS Credentials
vulnerabilities.description: Linux user accounts have trivially guessable passwords.

vulnerabilities.name: Privilege Escalation
vulnerabilities.description: Misconfigured sudo rule allows escalation to root.

containers.name: Database Server
containers.description: Internal PostgreSQL server holding patient records. Not directly reachable from outside.

vulnerabilities.name: Excessive Privileges
vulnerabilities.description: Database user has superuser rights beyond what the application needs.

containers.name: Nurse Workstation
containers.description: Internal staff workstation on the hospital network. Target for lateral movement.

vulnerabilities.name: Weak SSH Credentials
vulnerabilities.description: SSH user account has a trivially guessable password.

vulnerabilities.name: Sensitive Files Exposed
vulnerabilities.description: World-readable files contain plaintext credentials and internal notes.

vulnerabilities.name: SUID Misconfiguration
vulnerabilities.description: The find binary has the SUID bit set, enabling privilege escalation to root.
